title: ericameria cooperi var. cooperi
intro: Ericameria cooperi var. cooperi, also known as Cooper's goldenbush, is a shrub in the daisy family that is native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe wounds and reduce pain. The health benefits of Cooper's goldenbush include its ability to reduce fever, ease respiratory issues, and promote digestive health, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Cooper's goldenbush typically involve infusing the dried flowers and leaves in hot water to create a tea that can be consumed for its medicinal benefits.
